description: An MCP server that ignores close/shutdown signals and leaves orphaned child processes, open ports, or unflushed state.
body: Failure mode: when the host closes the connection or sends SIGTERM, the server aborts mid-call or never releases spawned processes, sockets, or temp files, so resources leak and the host hangs or accumulates zombies. Detection signals: orphaned child processes after host exit; ports still bound; in-flight tool calls truncated without cleanup. Do not rely on the host force-killing the process for cleanup. Safer fallback: handle shutdown signals, drain or cancel in-flight calls with a deadline, release all handles and child processes, then exit cleanly; verify with a shutdown leak test. Risk boundary: flag a server that cannot shut down cleanly rather than shipping it.
